codeReview.summary: The implementation is complete and well-tested. All six model management methods (get_model, list_models, create_model, update_model, delete_model, count_models) are properly implemented with comprehensive unit tests. The code follows established patterns from previous features, handles edge cases correctly, and includes proper error handling. All 31 unit tests pass successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created a temporary check_schema.py file during development to inspect the database schema, then properly cleaned it up in a separate commit. This pattern of creating temporary diagnostic scripts could be documented as a recommended practice in AGENTS.md for database-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Create check_schema.py, then later 'git add check_schema.py && git commit -m "chore: remove temporary schema check script"'. The cleanup commit (0c83f05) shows the file was properly removed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the mission-worker-base skill procedure correctly. The feature had no skillName specified in features.json, but the worker invoked mission-worker-base and followed TDD approach (tests first, then implementation, then verification). The handoff notes suggest assigning backend-worker skill to similar features for consistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: 'Tool: Skill { "skill": "mission-worker-base" }' at the start. Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges includes: 'The feature had no skillName specified, so I followed the mission-worker-base procedure directly. Consider assigning backend-worker skill to similar database implementation features for consistency.'

summary:
Feature m1-f6-model-methods successfully implements all six model management methods in the Database class. The implementation:

✓ Adds get_model(), list_models(), create_model(), update_model(), delete_model(), count_models() methods
✓ Follows established patterns from account/API key/admin user methods
✓ Handles the models table schema correctly with UNIQUE(provider_type, model_id) constraint
✓ Properly converts boolean enabled parameter to integer for database storage
✓ Manages timestamps automatically (created_at, updated_at)
✓ Supports filtering by provider_type and enabled_only
✓ Includes comprehensive unit tests (31 tests covering all CRUD operations, filtering, edge cases)
✓ All tests pass successfully

The feature fulfills assertions m1-a5 (Database class provides domain-specific methods) and m1-a8 (reduces code duplication). No blocking issues found. The worker properly cleaned up temporary files and followed TDD methodology.
